title: Crispy Bitter Peruvian Ground Beef Bake
description: A one‑pot Peruvian bake that pairs smoky ground beef with a bright, bitter aji amarillo sauce and a crunchy corn‑tortilla topping.

ingredients.name: aji amarillo paste
ingredients.notes: adds bitter heat; substitute with roasted yellow pepper if unavailable

instructions: In a large skillet, heat sesame oil over medium heat; add ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon; season with salt and pepper.
Stir in aji amarillo paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add black beans and quinoa to the skillet; mix well until everything is combined and heated through.
Transfer the mixture to a 9×13-inch baking dish, spreading evenly.
Sprinkle crushed corn tortilla chips over the top for a crispy finish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25–30 minutes, or until the top is golden and crisp.
Remove from oven and squeeze fresh lime juice over the bake.
Let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve warm.
